data.title: Binance to Launch DOLO Locked Products with 200% APR Promotion
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to introduce a new promotion for DOLO Simple Earn Locked Products, offering an exclusive 200% annual percentage rate (APR) for a limited period. This initiative is part of Binance's latest token listing for DOLO, and the promotion will run from 2025-08-29 00:00 (UTC) to 2025-09-11 23:59 (UTC). Eligible users can subscribe to these locked products and enjoy the high APR rewards for seven days.

To participate in the promotion, users must meet specific criteria during the promotion period. New Binance users who complete identity verification or existing verified users who achieve a minimum spot trading volume of $50 equivalent are eligible. It is important to note that it may take up to 48 hours for eligible users to access and subscribe to the special offer. The seven-day offer begins on the day users subscribe to the DOLO Locked Products, and they can still receive the 200% APR rewards even if the offer period extends beyond the promotion period.

The subscription process follows a first-come, first-served basis, with specific terms and conditions applying. Rewards are distributed daily to users' Spot Accounts, starting two days after subscription. The APR rewards are calculated based on the subscription amount, APR, and locked period. Users must adhere to minimum and maximum subscription limits, which impact the APR rewards received. The promotion is part of a sponsored partnership with Binance's token partner, allowing for a competitive APR offering. Users can buy or deposit DOLO on Binance and subscribe to the locked products to benefit from the promotion. Binance reserves the right to amend the terms and conditions of the promotion at its discretion.

data.title: Binance Resolves Futures Trading Outage, Confirms Full Service Restoration
data.description: Binance confirmed that USDⓈ-M Futures trading experienced a temporary outage on Friday. The issue has been fully resolved, with all futures markets back online. User funds remained secure, and the platform emphasized reliability as its top priority.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Cycle Slows, Expected to Rise by Fall 2025
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, CryptoQuant analyst Crypto Dan has observed a slowdown in the cryptocurrency market cycle, with an upward trend anticipated by fall 2025. Analyzing the proportion of Bitcoin held for over a year, based on realized market value, past cycles (first and second stages) have shown significant market surges reaching peaks.

However, in the current phase (third stage), the upward trend's slope is gradually flattening, and the cycle is becoming longer.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Sees Significant Inflows and Outflows
data.description: According to BlockBeats, recent data from Coinglass reveals notable movements in the cryptocurrency market over the past 24 hours. The net inflow of funds into various cryptocurrencies is led by SOL, which saw an influx of $116 million. Other significant inflows include USD1 with $46 million, EUR with $8.6 million, BNB with $7 million, and W with $4.75 million.Conversely, the market also experienced substantial outflows. Bitcoin (BTC) recorded a net outflow of $325 million, while Ethereum (ETH) saw $252 million leaving the market. XRP experienced a $90 million outflow, and PYTH saw $39 million exiting the market. These figures highlight the dynamic nature of cryptocurrency investments and the shifting preferences of investors.

data.title: Unicoin Challenges SEC Fraud Allegations in Court
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, crypto investment platform Unicoin has responded to the Securities and Exchange Commission's (SEC) fraud lawsuit, three months after it was filed. Unicoin has accused the SEC of misrepresenting its regulatory statements to build a case against the company. In a filing to a New York federal judge, Unicoin requested the dismissal of the lawsuit, arguing that the SEC selectively used parts of communications, misinterpreted routine financial projections as fraudulent, and ignored the company's warnings about potential risks. Unicoin further claimed that the SEC distorted disclosures made in its own filings to suggest deception.

The SEC's lawsuit, filed in May, accused Unicoin, its CEO Alex Konanykhin, board member Silvina Moschini, and former investment chief Alex Dominguez of raising $100 million by misleading investors about certificates that purportedly offered rights to receive Unicoin tokens and stock. Unicoin contended that the SEC's allegations lacked sufficient proof and that securities fraud requires a false statement made with intent to deceive, which reasonable investors would rely on. The company argued that since the risks identified by the SEC were openly disclosed, the elements of fraud could not be established.

Unicoin described the SEC's lawsuit as a "shotgun pleading" that failed to provide a motive for the alleged actions and relied on circumstantial evidence and mischaracterizations. The SEC alleged that Unicoin misled investors by claiming that its upcoming token and rights certificates were backed by billions of dollars in real-world assets, such as real estate and equity in pre-IPO companies. The regulator asserted that the actual value of these assets was significantly lower than claimed and that Unicoin misrepresented its financial situation.

In its defense, Unicoin argued that the SEC's claims were based on statements taken out of context, noting that executives had stated the company was asset-backed, not the tokens themselves. Unicoin emphasized that no defendant claimed the tokens would function as fully collateralized investments. The company also pointed out that the tokens had not yet been created and accused the SEC of penalizing it for optimistic forward-looking statements. Unicoin stated that the SEC's lawsuit had hindered its ability to mint tokens and back them with assets, while the lawsuit sought to hold it accountable for not creating fully collateralized tokens. Unicoin requested the court to dismiss the SEC's lawsuit with prejudice, preventing the complaint from being re-filed.

data.title: CMC Altcoin Season Index Stands at 58
